content.text: Kinematics is the branch of physics that deals with the motion of objects without considering the forces causing the motion.
Distance: Total path length (Scalar, always positive). Displacement: Shortest distance between initial and final position (Vector, can be zero/positive/negative).

content.name: Speed & Velocity
content.note: Speed is scalar; Velocity is vector.

content.name: Acceleration Formula
content.note: u = initial velocity, v = final velocity, t = time. Vector quantity.

content.name: Kinematic Equations
content.formula: v = u + at \\ s = ut + \frac{1}{2}at^2 \\ v^2 = u^2 + 2as

content.name: Gravity Equations
content.formula: v = u + gt \\ s = ut + \frac{1}{2}gt^2 \\ v^2 = u^2 + 2gs

content.name: Centripetal Acceleration
content.note: Acceleration acts towards the center in circular motion.
